title: Cooking Classes - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Cooking Classes 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS ENVIRONMENTAL CENTER STARKER ARTS GARDEN FOR EDUCATION FAMILY PROGRAMMING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers garden, cooking, and nutrition education classes. 4485 SW Country Club Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 753-9211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits.
